summary: Implemented remaining lbshadow.c work in one library file: lbShadow_8000E9F0 replaces a stub with HSD_Spline Vec3 derivative/tangent-style calculations for spline->type cases 0-3, and lbShadow_8000F38C replaces a large stub with the fighter shadow update/render pass that selects an HSD_LObj light, computes light/up vectors, manages LbShadow flags and active state, attaches fighter/accessory objects to HSD_Shadow, sets shadow camera/viewing rect/intensity, renders offscreen, and restores draw state. The PR body and review history provide little rationale beyond tracking; conclusions are primarily diff-based.

key_files.change: Added 317 lines and deleted 4; implemented lbShadow_8000E9F0 and lbShadow_8000F38C, added required baselib/ft/ground/vector/math includes, and removed stale placeholder comments.

what_changed: Added includes for MSL/math_ppc.h, baselib initialize/lobj/spline/video, cm types, ftdrawcommon, ft types, ground, and lbvector to support the new shadow and spline code.

Replaced the lbShadow_8000E9F0 placeholder with code that validates u in [0,1], maps u to a spline control-point segment, handles the u == 1.0 endpoint for type 0, and writes a Vec3 result using spline->cv and spline->tension formulas for type cases 0-3.
Removed a stale duplicate placeholder comment for lbShadow_8000EE8C after the function body already existed.

Replaced the lbShadow_8000F38C placeholder with a full per-fighter shadow pass: clears LbShadow x0_b2, finds a light object from HSD_GObj_804D7824[4] with fallback handling, asserts/loads light position, computes light direction/up vectors, and handles degenerate no-light cases.

lbShadow_8000F38C now iterates HSD_GObj_Entities->fighters, updates Fighter x20A4 shadow flags, deletes/re-adds shadow objects for visible fighters and accessories, toggles HSD_ShadowSetActive based on LbShadow x0_b0..x0_b5 and ftLib checks, and renders each valid shadow through HSD_StartRender/HSD_ShadowStartRender/HSD_ShadowEndRender.
smart_moves: Used existing engine APIs instead of open-coding subsystems: HSD_LObjGetPosition/GetInterest, lbVector helpers, HSD_Shadow object management, HSD_CObj camera setters, HSD_ViewingRect helpers, and ftDrawCommon render-state guards.

Preserved defensive engine behavior with HSD_ASSERT for missing light objects, HSD_ASSERTREPORT for missing light position, a noLight fallback when light distance is nearly zero, and an ortho fallback when viewing rect fitting fails after 0x14 attempts.
Kept partially understood data as offset/bitfield names such as x20A4, x0_b0, x0_b2, x0_b3, x21FC_flag.b7, and x221E_b5 rather than inventing semantics not proven by the slice.
Matched shadow activation to existing flags by computing anyFlag across x0_b0 through x0_b5 and only activating when no flags are set and ftLib_800872BC(gobj) passes.
decomp_lessons: For HSD_Spline-related decomp, preserve the exact segment computation pattern: t = u * (numcv - 1), idx = (s16) t, then t -= (f32) idx; endpoint handling can be special-cased instead of hidden in helper code.

Shadow/camera decomp often needs explicit degenerate-vector branches: this code separately handles near-zero total distance and near-vertical light vectors before building an orthonormal up vector with cross products.

When semantics are incomplete, stable address-based functions and offset fields are preferable to speculative names; this PR leaves lbShadow_8000E9F0/lbShadow_8000F38C and many Fighter/LbShadow bitfields address/offset-based.

Rendering passes should preserve state pairing visible in the engine: ftDrawCommon_80081200 before the fighter shadow loop, ftDrawCommon_80081168 after it, HSD_StartRender(HSD_RP_OFFSCREEN) before shadow rendering, and HSD_Init_803755A8 after each shadow render.

Nested scopes and repeated local aliases can be legitimate matching tools in large C decomp functions, especially around fighter user_data, temporary shadow objects, and loop-local viewing-rect variables.
naming_conventions: Retains doldecomp address-style names for unresolved functions: lbShadow_8000E9F0, lbShadow_8000F38C, and calls such as ftLib_800872B0/ftLib_800872BC.
Uses the lbShadow camel-prefix for library shadow functions while keeping type names in established style: LbShadow, HSD_Shadow, HSD_GObj, HSD_LObj, HSD_ViewingRect.
Local Vec3 names are semantic and role-based where evidence is clear: lightPos, lightDir, upVec, lightVec, rightVec, normDir, eyePos, interestPos, camPos.
Unknown or only partially understood struct fields remain offset/bitfield names: Fighter x20A4, x20A0_accessory, x21FC_flag.b7, x221E_b5, LbShadow x0_b0 through x0_b5.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK(16) in lbShadow_8000E9F0 and PAD_STACK(0x48) in lbShadow_8000F38C, indicating explicit stack-shape matching.
Preserved expression ordering and temporary variables for float math, including literals such as 0.001f, 0.0000010000001f, 100.0f, 1.2f, 128.0f, and loop limit 0x14.
Used sqrtf from MSL/math_ppc.h and vector helper calls lbVector_Diff, lbVector_Normalize, lbVector_CrossprodNormalized, and PSVECAdd rather than algebraically simplifying the math.
Kept nested blocks/redeclared locals such as fp2, hasObj, shadow2, i, scale, top, and bot, which likely helps reproduce compiler lifetimes/register allocation.
Preserved assert macro details including HSD_ASSERT line numbers 0x181/0x184 and __assert("shadow.h", 0x63, "shadow").
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided slice; the PR body only says "Tracking PR as per usual".
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or document LbShadow x0_b0 through x0_b5 and Fighter x20A4 shadow fields.
Search for HSD_Spline evaluation/derivative helpers to confirm semantic names for spline->type cases 0-3.
Look for subsequent changes to lbShadow_8000F38C, HSD_ViewingRectAddRect, or HSD_ShadowSetViewingRect for matching refinements.
Check whether the unusual lobj traversal/fallback pattern in HSD_GObj_804D7824[4] was preserved, simplified, or explained in later PRs.
